title: How Buffers Stabilize pH
script_summary: Buffers are essential for maintaining stable pH levels in blood and other solutions.

segments.voiceover: Buffers resist pH swings — blood chemistry depends on them.
Imagine if your blood's pH fluctuated wildly. Life would be impossible.
Buffers, like secret agents, maintain stability by neutralizing acids and bases.
For instance, bicarbonate in blood keeps pH around 7.4, crucial for cellular function.
Without buffers, even a slight change could lead to serious health issues. Think about that!
